---
output: html_document
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---
Lauren Shohan & Cecilia Liang
April.1st


## Set up
```{r}
#Load/install required package here
library(lubridate)
library(ggplot2)
library(forecast)  
library(Kendall)
library(tseries)
library(outliers)
library(tidyverse)
library(cowplot)
library(forecast)
library(trend)
library(readxl)

```
#Average down to daily data 
```{r}
#For ceclia
#humidity <- read_excel("~/R/ShohanLiang_ENV797_TSA_ForecastCompetition_S26/relative_humidity.xlsx")
#View(humidity) 

#temperature <- read_excel("~/R/ShohanLiang_ENV797_TSA_ForecastCompetition_S26/temperature.xlsx")
#View(temperature)

#load <- read_excel("~/R/ShohanLiang_ENV797_TSA_ForecastCompetition_S26/load.xlsx")
#View(load)
```

```{r}
#For laurens computer
humidity <- read_excel("~/Competition/ShohanLiang_Competition/relative_humidity.xlsx")
View(humidity) 

temperature <- read_excel("~/Competition/ShohanLiang_Competition/temperature.xlsx")
View(temperature)

load <- read_excel("~/Competition/ShohanLiang_Competition/load.xlsx")
View(load)
```

```{r}
load_daily <- load %>%
  select(date, h1:h24) %>%                        
  pivot_longer(cols = h1:h24,                      
               names_to = "hour",
               values_to = "load") %>%
  group_by(date) %>%                               
  summarise(daily_load = mean(load, na.rm = TRUE))
```

```{r}
# Temperature: pivot weather stations long, then average by date
temp_daily <- temperature %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("t_ws"),
               names_to  = "station",
               values_to = "temp") %>%
  group_by(date) %>%
  summarise(daily_temp = mean(temp, na.rm = TRUE))

# Humidity: same pattern with rh_ws columns
humi_daily <- humidity %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("rh_ws"),
               names_to  = "station",
               values_to = "humidity") %>%
  group_by(date) %>%
  summarise(humi_daily = mean(humidity, na.rm = TRUE))

```


```{r}
daily_data <- load_daily %>%
  left_join(temp_daily, by = "date") %>%
  left_join(humi_daily,  by = "date") %>%
  arrange(date)

View(daily_data)
summary(daily_data)
```

#Create time series object
```{r}
load_msts <- msts(daily_data$daily_load,
                  seasonal.periods = c(7, 365.25),
                  start = c(2005, 1)) 

View(load_msts)

train_full <- daily_data %>% filter(date <= as.Date("2011-06-30"))

train_full_msts <- msts(train_full$daily_load,
                        seasonal.periods = c(7, 365.25),
                        start = c(2005, 1))
```

```{r}
nrow(train_full)        
       
sum(is.na(daily_data$daily_load))   

# Plot to visually inspect
plot(train_full_msts, main = "Daily Load - Training Data")
```


# new model 2 -  Neural Network Autoregression (NNETAR)
# Fits a feed-forward neural net with lagged inputs + seasonal lags.
# p = AR lags, P = seasonal lags (one per seasonal period in msts).
# repeats = 20 averages over multiple random initializations for stability.

```{r}
TBATS_fit   <- tbats(train_full_msts)
TBATS_for   <- forecast(TBATS_fit, h = 31)

autoplot(TBATS_for) +
  ggtitle("NNETAR Forecast - July 2010") +
  xlab("Date") + ylab("Daily Load")
```

# trying to output
```{r}
# ── Build one submission file per model ──────────────────────────────────────
forecast_dates <- seq(as.Date("2011-07-01"), as.Date("2011-07-31"), by = "day")

# Change the $mean to whichever model you want to submit
submission <- data.frame(
  date = forecast_dates,
  load = as.numeric(TBATS_for$mean)   # swap for STLF_fit$mean or NNETAR_for$mean
)

write.csv(submission,
          file = "~/Competition/ShohanLiang_Competition/submission_TBATS_3.csv",
          row.names = FALSE,
          )
```
